title: Floaters documentation

examination.text: Supports detailed symptom and risk-factor history, anterior-vitreous review, dilated fundal examination, written information, local-protocol referral, and complete records.
examination.rationale: Supports symptom, risk-factor, vitreous and dilated-fundal examination records for floaters; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports detailed symptom and risk-factor history, anterior-vitreous review, dilated fundal examination, written information, local-protocol referral, and complete records.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice: Examining patients who present with flashes and floaters

exact_section.heading: Principles A286–A291 — history, vitreous and fundal examination, records

referral.text: Supports referral for retinal detachment, symptomatic retinal breaks or tears, pigment or haemorrhage, and safety-net information about new retinal-break or detachment symptoms.
referral.rationale: Supports retinal break or detachment referral and safety-net context for floaters; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports referral for retinal detachment, symptomatic retinal breaks or tears, pigment or haemorrhage, and safety-net information about new retinal-break or detachment symptoms.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Eye, onset, number and pattern, flashes, curtain or field loss, trauma, myopia, surgery, acuity, dilated examination and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for floaters.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
